name: Patient Case
history: Recent shoulder surgery with a painful recovery process. Ongoing pain, numbness, and tingling related to a herniated cervical disc. Prior history of physical therapy for past walking difficulties and back issues. Past prolonged illness associated with a period of immobility. Started a new relationship and later experienced a return of physical symptoms.
intermediate_belief: I cannot control my physical health.
intermediate_belief_depression: My physical condition will never improve.
coping_strategies: Avoidance of physical activities that might exacerbate pain.
situation: Received an MRI report indicating a herniated disc. Contacted the therapist after receiving the report.
auto_thought: I will never get better, and this pain will never go away.
behavior: Seeking reassurance from medical professionals and therapists.
